column2: eBay instrument: Large 50" African Kora-style gourd harp. Capture before disassembly. Units: inches.

column3: Main capture sheet, organized by subsystem. Fill the yellow 'Measured (in)' column. The blue 'Repo Target (in)' column shows the value from your own build spec (kora-design-table / CAD) for comparison - it is NOT what this instrument 'should' be, just a reference point.
One row per string. Record speaking length, total length, caliper diameter, which bank (left/right of bridge), and the tuning-ring position. Reference lengths/notes are from your design.md schedule.
Shot list for documenting condition and geometry. Tick 'Done' and note the filename so photos map to measurements.
Record what you find and the planned action per component. Your stated scope (cowskin, strings, tacks, anchor, sanding, finish) is pre-listed.

Define a single reference point and direction before measuring so everything is repeatable. Suggested: neck-to-gourd entry point = 0, measure UP the neck toward the head and DOWN toward the tail/anchor separately. Note your datum in the Notes.
String tension distorts the skin, bridge lean, and neck. Capture loaded geometry first (bridge height, skin doming, neck angle), then re-measure unloaded after removing strings.
For anything load-bearing or tapered (neck/handle/crossbar diameters, gourd diameter), measure in 2-3 places and record all; note location. Diameters are rarely round - record max and min.
Calipers for diameters, thicknesses, spacing, string gauges. Tape for lengths and the gourd circumference. Record string gauges in thousandths of an inch.
Before removing the skin, trace the gourd rim outline, the bridge footprint, and the tack pattern onto paper or photograph against a ruler. These are nearly impossible to reconstruct later.
Photograph the bridge straight-on and number the strings/notches L and R before loosening anything. Note the order they attach at the anchor.
Bag and label tacks, rings, and the bridge/foot. Note original tack spacing and count - your new tack pattern should match unless you decide otherwise.
Reference value from your repo build spec - read-only comparison.
